svg.text: URDU
NASTALIQ SCRIPT
NASTALIQ — HANGING CALLIGRAPHY
Urdu written in Nastaliq: most elegant Arabic-script style
Nastaliq = "hanging" — letters hang diagonally rightward
Developed in Persia (~15th c.); perfected in Mughal India
Right-to-left; 36 base letters; abjad (vowels optional)
URDU-SPECIFIC LETTERS
Added letters not in Arabic: ٹ /ʈ/ ڈ /ɖ/ ڑ /ɼ/ ں /ŋ/ ھ /ʰ/
These represent retroflex sounds from Sanskrit heritage
do chashmi he (ھ): aspirate marker in Nastaliq
SAMPLE TEXT
آپ کیسے ہیں؟ "Aap kaise hain?" (How are you? formal)
شکریہ "Shukriya" (Thank you)
اردو Urdu (the language)
URDU vs. HINDI — ONE SPOKEN LANGUAGE
Urdu and Hindi are mutually intelligible in everyday speech
Urdu: Nastaliq script • Hindi: Devanagari script
Urdu: Persian/Arabic vocabulary layer • Hindi: Sanskrit vocabulary
SINDHI · سنڌي · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
SINDHI PERSO-ARABIC SCRIPT (52 LETTERS)
Largest Perso-Arabic alphabet of any language (52 letters!)
Standard Arabic has 28 • Urdu Nastaliq has 36 • Sindhi has 52
Extra letters represent unique Sindhi phonemes:
ڌ /ɓ/ ڄ /ɗ/ ڃ /ɲ/ ڳ /ŋ/ (implosives + velar nasal)
SINDHI ALSO IN DEVANAGARI
In India: Hindu Sindhis use Devanagari for Sindhi
Same language, two scripts (like Urdu/Hindi!)
Pakistan Sindhi: Perso-Arabic • India Sindhi: Devanagari
SCRIPT COMPARISON
Both: right-to-left Perso-Arabic scripts
Urdu: 36 letters • Sindhi: 52 letters (more phonemic coverage)
Both: abjad system (short vowels usually omitted)
Shared Arabic letter base; different supplementary letters
PLATE 54a ALT · URDU / SINDHI · SCRIPT
